stocks.recent_news: Q4 FY26 results released May 8, 2026: consolidated revenue +18% YoY, India business 16% underlying volume growth. Sampann grew 69%, Tata Copper Plus +33%, Starbucks +7% with third consecutive quarter of positive same-store sales (+5%). Board recommended Rs 10/share final dividend for FY26 (TDS forms due May 25). CMP Rs 1,176.20 with market cap Rs 1.16 lakh cr. Approved third tranche of 3,25,020 Performance Share Units under SLTI Scheme 2024.
stocks.sector_context: Indian FMCG sector forecast 6-8% revenue growth in FY26 with 4-6% volume growth, supported by urban demand recovery, rural strength (rural volumes +7.7% vs urban +3.7% in recent quarter), stable commodity prices, GST adjustments, and RBI rate cuts. Tea/coffee input costs elevated but manageable; Nestle described 2026 commodity costs as 'sober'.
stocks.peer_comparison: Broad-based 16% UVG materially outperforms typical Indian FMCG peers running 4-6% volume growth. Innovation portfolio (Sampann, Tata Copper Plus) and Starbucks SSSG turnaround give it a stronger growth narrative than HUL, Nestle India, or Britannia.
red_flags: Performance Share Unit grants cause minor dilution over time
Stock has been a relative laggard in price action despite strong fundamentals - re-rating not yet visible
catalysts: Six investor conferences scheduled May 26 - June 8, 2026
Rs 10 final dividend payment subject to AGM approval
Continued Starbucks SSSG momentum after third positive quarter
Potential GST rate adjustments could boost FMCG demand

stocks.recent_news: Announced $11.75 billion acquisition of US-based Organon on April 27, 2026 - biopharma's largest deal of 2026; stock jumped 7% on the news. As of May 6, exploring Eurobonds via Organon's European entity and bond swaps as financing options. Funding mix: $2-2.5B internal reserves and $9.25-9.75B debt. CMP ~Rs 1,747 (April 28). Interim dividend of Rs 11 per share declared for FY26. Q4 FY26 earnings: May 21, 2026.
stocks.sector_context: Nifty Pharma down only 3% YTD vs ~12% drop in benchmark - defensive bet in 2026. Indian pharma market grew 12.4% YoY in February 2026 (third straight month of double-digit growth). US imposed 100% tariff on branded/patented pharma imports effective Oct 1, 2025, but generics (Indian pharma's main US franchise) remain exempt.
stocks.peer_comparison: Largest Indian drugmaker with 57 approved NDAs and 14 pending. Organon deal positions Sun as a global pharma leader but moves it from net cash to net debt/EBITDA of 2.3x - significantly more leveraged than Cipla, Dr Reddy's, or Lupin. Stock has lagged Torrent Pharma's run over the past year.
red_flags: Net debt/EBITDA jumps to ~2.3x post-Organon (from net cash)
Organon revenues expected to remain largely flat in 2026 - limited near-term accretion
Higher borrowing costs and integration execution risk
Funding mix (Eurobonds, bond swaps) still being finalized - terms uncertain
Halol facility USFDA overhang continues
catalysts: Up to $350M cost synergies targeted over 2-4 years from Organon
Closing of Organon transaction and financing details

stocks.recent_news: JB Chemicals merger advancing - Torrent shareholders approved scheme of amalgamation via NCLT-convened meeting on April 28, 2026. Torrent now holds 48.80% of JB Chemicals; exchange ratio is 51 Torrent shares per 100 JB shares (deal value Rs 25,689 cr). CMP Rs 4,380.80 (May 8) with market cap Rs 1.48 lakh cr. Q3 FY26 net profit +26.24% YoY to Rs 635 cr; Q3 dividend Rs 29/share declared. Q4 FY26 earnings: May 21, 2026.
stocks.sector_context: Indian pharma defensive in 2026 (Nifty Pharma -3% YTD vs benchmark -12%). Domestically-focused players favored amid US tariff uncertainty. Indian pharma market growing 12%+ on price hikes and new launches. Generics remain tariff-exempt; branded patented imports tariffed at 100%.
stocks.peer_comparison: Standout outperformer: ~39% one-year return vs Nifty Pharma ~14%. Post-merger Torrent will become 2nd-largest Indian pharma player. Q3 profit growth of 26% YoY outpaces most large-cap pharma peers. Strong domestic branded formulations franchise differentiates from US-generics-heavy peers (Sun, Dr Reddy's, Lupin).
red_flags: Merger still awaits final SEBI consents and court confirmations
Whalesbook flagged 'regulatory headwinds' alongside the growth story
Rich valuation after 39% one-year run sets a high bar going into Q4 print
Increased post-merger debt to service the JB Chemicals consolidation
catalysts: JB Chemicals merger final approvals and closure
Continued domestic IPM share gains and semaglutide ramp

stocks.recent_news: Q4 FY26 results: net profit +19% YoY to Rs 279 cr, revenue +5% to Rs 1,812 cr, EBITDA +10% to Rs 523 cr (margin 28.9%) - driven by CDMO growth and margin expansion. Stock hit all-time high of Rs 1,186.80 on May 6, 2026; trading near Rs 1,219.70 on May 7. Ex-dividend May 8 (Rs 1.20/share, payable May 30). Appointed Dr. Shekhar Mande and Ms. Sutapa Banerjee as independent directors effective July 2, 2026 (Aruna Bhinge retiring July 6).
stocks.sector_context: Indian pharma sector defensive in 2026. CDMO segment is the bright spot with structural tailwinds from global pharma supply chain diversification. Generics tariff-exempt under Trump's October 2025 100% tariff on branded imports - favorable for Indian API/CDMO players.
stocks.peer_comparison: Stock outpacing broader market and pharma sector peers with strong momentum to all-time high. CDMO franchise provides growth profile differentiated from pure-generics peers (Dr Reddy's, Lupin); closer in profile to Divi's Labs and Syngene.
red_flags: Kotak Equities maintains 'Sell' rating with Rs 800 target - implies ~33% downside
Q4 EBITDA missed consensus by ~8% on weak FDF (formulations) segment
Stock at all-time high creates near-term profit-taking risk after strong run
Promoter pledge at 2.69% (December 2025) - low but should be tracked
catalysts: Dividend record date May 8, payment May 30 (Rs 1.20/share)
New independent director additions effective July 2, 2026
FY27 guidance and CDMO pipeline updates in upcoming concalls

stocks.recent_news: Q4 FY26 results (announced April 30, 2026): revenue +16.6% YoY to Rs 702 cr; net profit -15.1% YoY to Rs 132 cr (impacted by one-time Rs 30 cr labor code cost). Q4 EBITDA Rs 187 cr (margin 26.6%). Stock hit 20% upper band on May 4 on the strong revenue print despite profit decline. Completed acquisition of Shahbazkers Diagnostic Centre on May 1, 2026 (now sole owner). CMP Rs 1,325.40 (May 4), market cap Rs 22,908 cr.
stocks.sector_context: Indian diagnostics consolidating with top 3 (Dr Lal, Metropolis, SRL) controlling ~52% of market. Sector faces structural pricing pressure from disruptive online entrants. Metropolis aggressively expanding into tier 2/3 cities; Thyrocare grew Q4 PAT +88% YoY.
stocks.peer_comparison: Revenue growth of 16.6% solid but margin pressure visible. Thyrocare and Metropolis growing at similar/higher rates with better margin trajectory. Direct competitive threat from Tata 1mg's B2C diagnostics launch (Lipid Profile priced Rs 100 in Bengaluru vs Dr Lal's ~Rs 960 for the same test).
red_flags: Tata 1mg B2C diagnostics launch could compress incumbent profits by 40-50% per industry research
Q4 profit fell 15.1% YoY despite strong revenue growth - margin compression visible
ICICI Securities cut target price to Rs 1,460 post-Q4 citing margin pressure
Management guides FY27 EBITDA margin at 27-28% - no meaningful expansion expected
One-time labor code cost of Rs 30 cr signals new structural cost overhang
catalysts: Shahbazkers acquisition integration and synergy realization
FY27 guidance updates in upcoming concalls
Q1 FY27 results showing sustainability of revenue growth ex-one-time costs
